text.title: Process Mining Tool Shortlist Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules with rationale + source for process-mining-tool-shortlist. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Shortlist MUST score each tool against weighted criteria (ingestion fit, query language, visualisation, conformance check, conformance check, total cost of ownership, vendor lock-in); weights agreed with sponsor before scoring.
rule.rationale: MCDM (multi-criteria decision-making) practice — applies to Process Mining Tool Shortlist.

rule.statement: Each tool score MUST cite evidence: vendor demo recording, sample event log result, G2 reference call, hands-on POC; un-anchored scores forbidden.
rule.rationale: Vendor-evaluation research — applies to Process Mining Tool Shortlist.

rule.statement: Shortlist MUST contain at least 3 tools (commercial + open-source); single-tool shortlists are not shortlists.
rule.rationale: Decision-quality practice — applies to Process Mining Tool Shortlist.

rule.statement: Each shortlist row MUST have a single named owner who ran the evaluation; consensus authorship is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Single-owner accountability — applies to Process Mining Tool Shortlist.

rule.statement: Shortlist carries a review-by date (default 6 months); past that, vendor landscape may have shifted enough to invalidate.
rule.rationale: Operational hygiene — applies to Process Mining Tool Shortlist.

rule.statement: Final pick is recorded as an ADR (architecture decision record) attached to the shortlist; tool-pick without ADR is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Decision-record practice — applies to Process Mining Tool Shortlist.

rule.statement: Rejected tools MUST list rejection reason; silent omission is forbidden because the next engagement re-derives the rejection.
rule.rationale: Decision-quality research — applies to Process Mining Tool Shortlist.

rule.statement: If tool choice is already mandated or the scope is below the methodology break-even, return `skipped`.
rule.rationale: Methodology applicability — applies to Process Mining Tool Shortlist.
